summary: iOS 👀 FALCON | DFU - App incorrectly shows device upto date after failure
scenario: After a DFU failure for the headset, the app incorrectly displays the device as up-to-date instead of showing the update failed.
description_clean: After a DFU (Device Firmware Update) failure for the headset, the app incorrectly displays the device as up-to-date. The headset was not actually updated, but the main SW update section shows it as successfully updated to the latest build. This misleads the user into thinking the update succeeded when it actually failed.
steps: 1. Initiate a DFU (Device Firmware Update) for the headset.
2. Cause the DFU process to fail (e.g., disconnect, interrupt, or simulate an error).
3. Observe the main SW update section in the app after the failure.
expected: The app should indicate that the DFU failed and show the device as not updated or on its previous firmware version.
actual: The app incorrectly displays the device as up-to-date and shows it as successfully updated to the latest build, misleading the user.
